reasoning: The asset in question is International Paper (IP), which has recently completed a major acquisition of DS Smith and pays a relatively high dividend yield of ~3.4%. Forecasting future stock prices compared to a fixed threshold (the unadjusted close on 2025-02-28, which was 54.06) involves balancing the equity risk premium against the dividend expected to drop the price, and the variance drag. The expected log-drift of the stock is approximately (mu - y - sigma^2/2). Assuming an equity premium (mu) around 8-9%, an annual dividend yield (y) of 3.4%, and volatility (sigma) of ~25%, the log-drift is only very slightly positive (around +1.5% to +2.5%). Consequently, the probability of the stock closing higher than its initial value begins near 50% for short time intervals (like 1 week and 1 month) and drifts slightly upward to around 52% over the span of a year. Short-term signals from Yahoo Finance's trend tool also suggest an approximately ~49% likelihood for the near term, keeping the baseline neutral. We thus submit slowly drifting probabilities very close to 0.50, reflecting high uncertainty for single-stock forecasting and minor positive drift over long horizons.
